steps.title: Spot the main topic
steps.content: A 2009 study concluded that ocean acidification affects fish behavior. The passage then examines concerns about this conclusion's reliability, focusing on sample size and potential bias.

steps.title: Match the topic to the answer choices
steps.content:
The passage discusses two studies (Dixson 2009; Clark 2022) both centered on how ocean acidification affects fish behavior — specifically the methodological concern that small sample sizes may overstate this effect. The main topic must capture this central subject.
  • Choice A — "Loss of marine diversity due to overfishing.": overfishing is never mentioned; the passage is about acidification, not species loss. ✗
  • Choice B — "Changes in technologies used to capture fish.": fishing technology is not discussed; the passage examines behavioral effects of acidification. ✗
  • Choice C — "Feeding patterns of top marine predators.": the passage names Amphiprion percula (clownfish, not a top predator) and never discusses feeding patterns. ✗
  • Choice D — "Effects of ocean acidification on fish behavior.": this is exactly what both cited studies investigate — Dixson's 2009 study, Clark's 2022 methodological review of it, and the broader discussion all center on this question. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
